summary:
Absent deep tendon reflexes do not occur in non-paralytic polio; instead, it may have preserved reflexes or even slightly increased tone from retained motor function. Head drop and nuchal rigidity indicate broader neurological impact beyond typical spinal cord inflammation associated with this form of poliomyelitis.
The answer is \(\boxed{\text{A}}\). Agent2's reasoning can be summarized into two concise sentences focusing on critical aspects related to distinguishing between different forms of poliovirus infection:

Absence of deep tendon reflexes indicates complete nerve damage which doesn't align with non-paralytic polio where some neural functions often persist. Other symptoms such as head drop and neck stiffness point towards wider central nervous system effects inconsistent with just localized spasticity found in non-paralytic cases. 

Thus,
\[ 
\boxed{\text{A}}
\]
